message.content:
Create a detailed implementation plan for validating the package upgrades in the Belt Tools project.

## Context from Exploration

### Package Upgrades (commit a3552f7)
Critical changes that need verification:
1. **@types/node**: 22.x → 25.x (MAJOR) - Type definitions changed
2. **immer**: 10.2.0 → 11.1.3 (MAJOR) - State machine depends on this
3. **turbo**: 2.3.0 → 2.7.2
4. **@biomejs/biome**: 2.3.8 → 2.3.11
5. **tsup**: 8.3.0 → 8.5.1
6. **vitest**: 4.0.8 → 4.0.16
7. **typebox**: 1.0.50 → 1.0.73

### TODOs/FIXMEs Status
- **NONE found** - codebase is clean

### Test Infrastructure
- Only 1 test file: `packages/tools/src/schema/lib/__tests__/type.spec.ts` (240 lines)
- No tests for state-machine or helpers
- Test commands: `pnpm test`, `pnpm test:watch`, `pnpm coverage`

### Files to Verify
```
packages/tools/src/
├── helpers/ (17 modules - NO TESTS)
│   ├── compose.ts
│   ├── create-proxy.ts
│   ├── date-utils.ts
│   ├── error-utils.ts
│   └── ...
├── schema/ (PARTIALLY TESTED)
│   ├── lib/type.ts ✓ TESTED
│   └── ...
└── state-machine/ (NO TESTS - uses Immer!)
    ├── machine.ts
    ├── slice.ts
    ├── hooks.ts
    └── ...
```

### Task Requirements
1. Execute ALL existing tests (unit tests)
2. Run build verification (`pnpm build`)
3. Run type checking (`pnpm typecheck`)
4. Run linting (`pnpm lint`)
5. Manual verification of state-machine (Immer upgrade is MAJOR)
6. Verify all exports work correctly

### Plan Requirements
- Detailed step-by-step verification process
- Risk assessment for each major upgrade
- Manual test procedures for untested modules
- Rollback strategy if issues found
- Expected output/results for each step

Create a comprehensive plan that ensures the package upgrades didn't break anything.

content.text: I'll explore the codebase to understand the current state and create a comprehensive validation plan for the package upgrades.

Links: [{"title":"Releases · immerjs/immer","url":"https://github.com/immerjs/immer/releases"},{"title":"immer: Versions | Openbase","url":"https://openbase.com/js/immer/versions"},{"title":"Release v11.0.0 · immerjs/immer","url":"https://github.com/immerjs/immer/releases/tag/v11.0.0"},{"title":"Version Specific Upgrades | CMS | Umbraco Documentation","url":"https://docs.umbraco.com/umbraco-cms/fundamentals/setup/upgrading/version-specific"},{"title":"Migration Guide 11.x to 12.0","url":"https://github.com/LuckyPennySoftware/MediatR/wiki/Migration-Guide-11.x-to-12.0"},{"title":"Migrate from ASP.NET Core in .NET 9 to ASP.NET Core in .NET 10 | Microsoft Learn","url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/aspnet/core/migration/90-to-100?view=aspnetcore-10.0"},{"title":"Breaking Changes in .NET 10: A Migration Guide from .NET 8","url":"https://www.gapvelocity.ai/blog/dotnet8-to-dotnet10-migration-guide"},{"title":"OutSystems 11 side effects and breaking changes","url":"https://success.outsystems.com/support/release_notes/11/outsystems_11_side_effects_and_breaking_changes/"},{"title":"Immich v1.102.0 - ⚠️ Breaking Changes (OPT-IN ONLY) - Lemmy.World","url":"https://lemmy.world/post/14492246"},{"title":"Upgrade to Episerver 10 - ted&gustaf","url":"https://tedgustaf.com/blog/2016/upgrade-to-episerver-10/"}]

Here are the search results for Immer 11 breaking changes and migration from version 10:

## Immer 11 Breaking Changes and Migration

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Immer 11:

### Release Information
Immer 11.0.0 was released on 2025-11-23 with performance improvements including a rewrite of the finalization system to use a callback approach instead of tree traversal.

### Breaking Changes in v11.0.0
The breaking change in version 11 is that it enables loose iteration by default.

### Migration from v10 to v11
Note that this slightly modifies the behavior of Immer with classes in obscure corner cases, in ways that match current documentation, but do not match previous behavior. If you run into issues with this release with class instances, use `setUseStrictShallowCopy("class_only")` to revert to the old behavior.

### Key Changes from v10
For reference, here are some important changes that were introduced in v10 (which you may have already addressed):

- Shortening the length of a JSON array now results in delete patches, rather than a mutation of the length property, in accordance with JSON spec.

- Immer is now an ESM package that can be directly imported into the browser. CJS should still work, UMD support has been removed.

- Replace all calls to `enableAllPlugins()` with `enablePatches(); enableMapSet();` to be more specific and smoothen future migrations.

- When using getters/setters with plain objects, call `useStrictShallowCopy(true)` at startup. Replace all default imports: Replace `import produce from "immer"` with `import {produce} from "immer"`.

For more detailed information, you can check the official Immer documentation at https://immerjs.github.io/immer/complex-objects#semantics-in-detail.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Here are the search results for Immer 11 breaking changes and migration from version 10:

## Immer 11 Breaking Changes and Migration

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Immer 11:

### Release Information
Immer 11.0.0 was released on 2025-11-23 with performance improvements including a rewrite of the finalization system to use a callback approach instead of tree traversal.

### Breaking Changes in v11.0.0
The breaking change in version 11 is that it enables loose iteration by default.

### Migration from v10 to v11
Note that this slightly modifies the behavior of Immer with classes in obscure corner cases, in ways that match current documentation, but do not match previous behavior. If you run into issues with this release with class instances, use `setUseStrictShallowCopy("class_only")` to revert to the old behavior.

### Key Changes from v10
For reference, here are some important changes that were introduced in v10 (which you may have already addressed):

- Shortening the length of a JSON array now results in delete patches, rather than a mutation of the length property, in accordance with JSON spec.

- Immer is now an ESM package that can be directly imported into the browser. CJS should still work, UMD support has been removed.

- Replace all calls to `enableAllPlugins()` with `enablePatches(); enableMapSet();` to be more specific and smoothen future migrations.

- When using getters/setters with plain objects, call `useStrictShallowCopy(true)` at startup. Replace all default imports: Replace `import produce from "immer"` with `import {produce} from "immer"`.

For more detailed information, you can check the official Immer documentation at https://immerjs.github.io/immer/complex-objects#semantics-in-detail.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about `@types/node` version 25 and changes from version 22:

## @types/node Version Information

The latest version of @types/node is 25.0.3, published 19 days ago.

## Important Note About @types/node Changelogs

@types/* packages do not follow SemVer at all. Breaking changes may occur at any time. You should thoroughly test the upgrade before applying.(no content)Finding a changelog for @types/node can be difficult - users have reported difficulty finding a changelog of breaking changes to follow and analyze when upgrading between versions.

## Node.js 25 Breaking Changes (which @types/node 25 reflects)

The `@types/node` package versions generally align with Node.js versions. Here are key breaking changes from Node.js 22 to 25:

### Removed APIs
fs.F_OK, fs.R_OK, fs.W_OK, fs.X_OK have been removed (SEMVER-MAJOR change).(no content)The fs module introduced a runtime deprecation for F_OK, R_OK, W_OK, and X_OK getters exposed directly on node:fs. Get them from fs.constants or fs.promises.constants instead.

### Other Breaking Changes
Additional SEMVER-MAJOR changes include: http.writeHeader deprecation, perf_hooks deprecated accessors moved to EOF, process.multipleResolves event moved to EOL, and tls IP-address servername deprecation moved to EOL.(no content)The release includes behavior and validation changes including stricter fetch() compliance, AbortSignal validation, stream/pipe errors now throwing, changes to Buffer behavior, path handling fixes on Windows, test runner defaults, and more.

### Platform Changes
Node.js no longer provides pre-built binaries for 32-bit Windows (x86) as of Node.js 23.0.0, and 32-bit Linux on armv7 as of Node.js 24.0.0.(no content)Pre-built binaries for macOS now require a minimum of macOS 13.5.

### V8 and Build Requirements
Addons linking against V8 APIs may need updates for V8 13.6; C++20 support may be required where previously C++17 was used.

For the complete list of changes in the type definitions themselves, you would need to check the [DefinitelyTyped repository's commit history](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/tree/master/types/node) directly, as there's no formal changelog maintained for @types packages.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Additional SEMVER-MAJOR changes include: http.writeHeader deprecation, perf_hooks deprecated accessors moved to EOF, process.multipleResolves event moved to EOL, and tls IP-address servername deprecation moved to EOL.(no content)The release includes behavior and validation changes including stricter fetch() compliance, AbortSignal validation, stream/pipe errors now throwing, changes to Buffer behavior, path handling fixes on Windows, test runner defaults, and more.

### Platform Changes
Node.js no longer provides pre-built binaries for 32-bit Windows (x86) as of Node.js 23.0.0, and 32-bit Linux on armv7 as of Node.js 24.0.0.(no content)Pre-built binaries for macOS now require a minimum of macOS 13.5.

### V8 and Build Requirements
Addons linking against V8 APIs may need updates for V8 13.6; C++20 support may be required where previously C++17 was used.

For the complete list of changes in the type definitions themselves, you would need to check the [DefinitelyTyped repository's commit history](https://github.com/DefinitelyTyped/DefinitelyTyped/tree/master/types/node) directly, as there's no formal changelog maintained for @types packages.

Based on the search results, here's a summary of TypeBox 1.0 breaking changes:

## TypeBox 1.0 Breaking Changes

This document covers upgrading from TypeBox 0.34.x to 1.0. While most of the API surface remains intact for 1.0, there are several key breaking changes that will impact users who integrate deeply with TypeBox infrastructure.

### Key Breaking Changes:

**1. ESM-Only Package**
Version 1.0 is published as an ESM-only package. Users will need to ensure their environments support ESM module resolution.

**2. Symbol Removal**
Version 1.0 removes the Kind, ReadonlyKind, OptionalKind symbols. These symbols were originally introduced to enable fast type differentiation internal to the library, as well as to solve integration issues with Ajv strict mode. The use of symbols have proven difficult for users who install multiple versions of TypeBox side by side. This is most notable in frameworks that install one version of TypeBox and where users install another.(no content)Version 1.0 replaces each symbol with non-enumerable ~kind, ~readonly and ~optional properties. Applications that use symbols to reflect TypeBox types will need to update to read these properties instead.

**3. Date and Uint8Array Types Removed**
Version 1.0 removes the Date and Uint8Array types. These types can be created at an application level by extending Type.Base.

**4. Compiler API Changes**
Version 1.0 makes API changes to the TypeBox compiler but retains the same functionality. The biggest change in 1.0 is the renaming of TypeCheck to Validator. Implementations that reference TypeCheck will need to updated reference Validator.(no content)Additionally, the TypeCompiler namespace has been deprecated, implementations can now use the Compile function exported on the submodule.

**5. Reference Handling Model**
Version 1.0 introduces a model for handling referential types. This model is reflected across Value and Compiler submodules. In 1.0, references are new passed via a optional Context object.

### Compatibility Note:
If upgrading from @sinclair/typebox 0.34.x refer to the 1.0 migration guide. Most types created with 0.34.x are compatible with V1, and it is possible to run both typebox and @sinclair/typebox packages side by side.

For the full migration guide, see: https://github.com/sinclairzx81/typebox/blob/main/changelog/1.0.0-migration.md


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

Based on the search results, here's a summary of TypeBox 1.0 breaking changes:

## TypeBox 1.0 Breaking Changes

This document covers upgrading from TypeBox 0.34.x to 1.0. While most of the API surface remains intact for 1.0, there are several key breaking changes that will impact users who integrate deeply with TypeBox infrastructure.

### Key Breaking Changes:

**1. ESM-Only Package**
Version 1.0 is published as an ESM-only package. Users will need to ensure their environments support ESM module resolution.

**2. Symbol Removal**
Version 1.0 removes the Kind, ReadonlyKind, OptionalKind symbols. These symbols were originally introduced to enable fast type differentiation internal to the library, as well as to solve integration issues with Ajv strict mode. The use of symbols have proven difficult for users who install multiple versions of TypeBox side by side. This is most notable in frameworks that install one version of TypeBox and where users install another.(no content)Version 1.0 replaces each symbol with non-enumerable ~kind, ~readonly and ~optional properties. Applications that use symbols to reflect TypeBox types will need to update to read these properties instead.

**3. Date and Uint8Array Types Removed**
Version 1.0 removes the Date and Uint8Array types. These types can be created at an application level by extending Type.Base.

**4. Compiler API Changes**
Version 1.0 makes API changes to the TypeBox compiler but retains the same functionality. The biggest change in 1.0 is the renaming of TypeCheck to Validator. Implementations that reference TypeCheck will need to updated reference Validator.(no content)Additionally, the TypeCompiler namespace has been deprecated, implementations can now use the Compile function exported on the submodule.

**5. Reference Handling Model**
Version 1.0 introduces a model for handling referential types. This model is reflected across Value and Compiler submodules. In 1.0, references are new passed via a optional Context object.

### Compatibility Note:
If upgrading from @sinclair/typebox 0.34.x refer to the 1.0 migration guide. Most types created with 0.34.x are compatible with V1, and it is possible to run both typebox and @sinclair/typebox packages side by side.

content.text:
Now I have enough information to create a comprehensive validation plan. Let me provide the detailed implementation plan:

---

# Implementation Plan: Package Upgrades Validation for Belt Tools

## Executive Summary

This plan validates the package upgrades in commit `a3552f7` which upgraded multiple dependencies. The most critical changes involve:

1. **immer**: 10.2.0 -> 11.1.3 (MAJOR) - Used extensively in state-machine
2. **typebox**: 1.0.50 -> 1.0.73 (PATCH but significant) - Core schema validation
3. **@types/node**: 22.x -> 25.x (MAJOR) - Type definitions
4. **Other upgrades**: turbo, biome, tsup, vitest (minor risk)

---

## Phase 1: Automated Verification Pipeline

### Step 1.1: Clean Installation
**Command:**
```bash
cd /Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt
rm -rf node_modules pnpm-lock.yaml packages/tools/node_modules
pnpm install
```

**Expected Results:**
- No peer dependency warnings for immer, typebox
- Clean installation without errors
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` regenerated successfully

**Risk Assessment:** LOW - Standard operation

---

### Step 1.2: Type Checking
**Command:**
```bash
pnpm typecheck
```

**Expected Results:**
- Zero TypeScript errors
- All types resolve correctly

**What to Watch For:**
- `@types/node` 25.x removed `fs.F_OK`, `fs.R_OK`, `fs.W_OK`, `fs.X_OK` - verify no direct usage
- TypeBox 1.0.x changed symbol properties to `~kind`, `~readonly`, `~optional`

**Risk Assessment:** MEDIUM - @types/node major version change

**Files to Verify if Errors:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/schema/lib/type.ts` - Uses `TSchema`, `Type`, `Compile`, `Value`
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/helpers/error-utils.ts` - Uses `Error.captureStackTrace`

---

### Step 1.3: Linting
**Command:**
```bash
pnpm lint
```

**Expected Results:**
- Zero linting errors
- Biome 2.3.11 should be backward compatible with 2.3.8

**Risk Assessment:** LOW - Patch version upgrade

---

### Step 1.4: Build Verification
**Command:**
```bash
pnpm build
```

**Expected Results:**
- `packages/tools/dist/` generated with:
  - `index.js` (ESM)
  - `index.cjs` (CommonJS)
  - `index.d.ts` (TypeScript declarations)
  - Source maps

**What to Watch For:**
- tsup 8.5.1 should handle ESM/CJS dual format correctly
- Tree-shaking should work with immer 11.x ESM-first approach

**Risk Assessment:** LOW - Minor version upgrade

---

### Step 1.5: Run Existing Tests
**Command:**
```bash
pnpm test
```

**Expected Results:**
- All 20+ tests in `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/schema/lib/__tests__/type.spec.ts` pass
- Coverage for TypeBox type validation functions

**Current Test Coverage:**
- `createBaseType` with primitives (string, number, boolean, null, undefined)
- `createBaseType` with complex types (objects, arrays, unions, optional)
- `errors` method validation
- `decode` method validation
- `createDateType` custom date validation
- `TypeBoxInfer` type inference

**Risk Assessment:** MEDIUM - TypeBox patch version may have behavioral changes

---

## Phase 2: Immer 11.x Deep Validation (CRITICAL)

### Background
Immer 11.x introduced breaking changes:
- Loose iteration enabled by default
- Finalization system rewritten (callback approach instead of tree traversal)
- Class instance behavior modified

### Step 2.1: Immer Usage Analysis

**Files Using Immer:**
1. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/state-machine/machine.ts`
   - Line 1: `import { produce } from 'immer';`
   - Line 35: `produce(state, (draft) => {...})` - GetSelectorsWrapper

2. `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/state-machine/slice.ts`
   - Line 1: `import { produce } from 'immer';`
   - Line 158: Async slice reducer
   - Line 205: Sync slice reducer

**Usage Pattern:** Only `produce()` function is used - no `createDraft`, `finishDraft`, `enableMapSet`, or `enablePatches`.

### Step 2.2: Manual State Machine Test

Since there are NO tests for state-machine, create a manual verification script:

```typescript
// Test script (run via Node.js REPL or temporary test file)
import { createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice } from '@belt/tools';

// Test 1: Sync slice with produce
const counterSlice = createSlice({
  id: 'counter',
  cache: { strategy: 'none' },
  initial: { count: 0 },
  reducers: {
    increment: (draft) => { draft.count += 1; },
    decrement: (draft) => { draft.count -= 1; },
    set: (draft, payload: number) => { draft.count = payload; },
  },
});

// Test 2: Async slice
const userSlice = createAsyncSlice({
  id: 'user',
  cache: { strategy: 'none' },
  context: {},
  load: async () => ({ name: 'Test User' }),
});

// Test 3: Create machine and dispatch
const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'testMachine',
  slices: { counter: counterSlice, user: userSlice },
});

// Verify state mutations
machine.dispatch(machine.actions.counter.increment(null));
console.assert(machine.getState().counter.count === 1, 'Increment failed');

machine.dispatch(machine.actions.counter.set(10));
console.assert(machine.getState().counter.count === 10, 'Set failed');

machine.dispatch(machine.actions.counter.decrement(null));
console.assert(machine.getState().counter.count === 9, 'Decrement failed');

console.log('All state machine tests passed!');
```

**Expected Results:**
- State mutations work correctly through `produce()`
- No "Cannot assign to read only property" errors
- Draft proxy behaves as expected

**Risk Assessment:** HIGH - No existing tests, core functionality

---

### Step 2.3: Immer Class Instance Verification

The breaking change notes mention class instance behavior. Check if any class instances are used in state:

**Analysis of State Structure:**
- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/state-machine/models/` - State is plain objects
- No class instances detected in initial state or reducers

**Risk Assessment:** LOW - No class instances in state

---

## Phase 3: TypeBox Validation (MEDIUM RISK)

### Step 3.1: TypeBox API Changes Analysis

**TypeBox 1.0.x Changes:**
1. ESM-only package
2. `~kind`, `~readonly`, `~optional` replace symbols
3. `TypeCheck` renamed to `Validator`
4. `TypeCompiler` deprecated, use `Compile` function

**Current Usage in `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/schema/lib/type.ts`:**
```typescript
import { type Static, type TSchema, Type } from 'typebox';
import { Compile } from 'typebox/compile';
import { Value } from 'typebox/value';
```

**Risk Assessment:** LOW - Already using new API (`Compile` not `TypeCompiler`)

### Step 3.2: Custom Date Type Verification

The code uses `Type.Unsafe` with custom `~customKind` property:
```typescript
export function createDateType(): TSchema {
  return Type.Unsafe<Date>({ '~customKind': CUSTOM_DATE_KIND });
}
```

**Test:**
```typescript
import { createBaseType, createDateType } from '@belt/tools';

const dateType = createBaseType(createDateType());
console.assert(dateType.check(new Date()) === true);
console.assert(dateType.check(new Date('invalid')) === false);
console.assert(dateType.check('2025-01-01') === false);
```

---

## Phase 4: @types/node 25 Verification

### Step 4.1: Check for Removed APIs

**Removed in Node.js 25:**
- `fs.F_OK`, `fs.R_OK`, `fs.W_OK`, `fs.X_OK`

**Search Command:**
```bash
grep -r "fs\.F_OK\|fs\.R_OK\|fs\.W_OK\|fs\.X_OK" packages/tools/src/
```

**Expected Result:** No matches (verified: no fs module usage in schema/helpers)

### Step 4.2: Error.captureStackTrace Compatibility

**File:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/helpers/error-utils.ts`

**Current Implementation:**
```typescript
export const hasCaptureStackTrace = typeof Error.captureStackTrace === 'function';

export function captureStackTrace(targetError: Error, constructorOpt?: Function): void {
  if (!hasCaptureStackTrace) return;
  if (isBun) {
    Error.captureStackTrace(targetError);
  } else {
    Error.captureStackTrace(targetError, constructorOpt);
  }
}
```

**Risk Assessment:** LOW - Already handles missing `captureStackTrace`

---

## Phase 5: Integration Verification

### Step 5.1: Full Export Verification

**Test all exports work:**
```typescript
import {
  // Helpers
  createProxy,
  formatDate,
  InvariantError,
  maskData,
  ms,
  objectKeys,
  capitalize,
  createSymbol,
  setTimeout,
  
  // Schema
  createType,
  createModel,
  
  // State Machine
  createMachine,
  createSlice,
  createAsyncSlice,
} from '@belt/tools';

// Verify each export is defined
const exports = [
  createProxy, formatDate, InvariantError, maskData, ms,
  objectKeys, capitalize, createSymbol, setTimeout,
  createType, createModel,
  createMachine, createSlice, createAsyncSlice,
];

exports.forEach((exp, i) => {
  console.assert(exp !== undefined, `Export ${i} is undefined`);
});
```

### Step 5.2: ESM/CJS Dual Build Verification

```bash
# Test ESM import
node --input-type=module -e "import('@belt/tools').then(m => console.log('ESM OK', Object.keys(m).length))"

# Test CJS require
node -e "console.log('CJS OK', Object.keys(require('@belt/tools')).length)"
```

---

## Phase 6: React Hooks Verification (Optional)

Since `hooks.ts` uses React:

```typescript
import React from 'react';
// Uses React.useSyncExternalStore, React.useRef
```

**Test (requires React environment):**
```tsx
import { createMachine, createSlice } from '@belt/tools';

const machine = createMachine({
  id: 'test',
  slices: {
    counter: createSlice({
      id: 'counter',
      cache: { strategy: 'none' },
      initial: { count: 0 },
      reducers: { increment: (d) => { d.count++; } },
    }),
  },
});

function TestComponent() {
  const { state, actions } = machine.useMachine('counter');
  return <button onClick={() => actions.increment(null)}>{state.count}</button>;
}
```

---

## Risk Matrix

| Package | Version Change | Risk Level | Reason |
|---------|---------------|------------|--------|
| immer | 10.2.0 -> 11.1.3 | **HIGH** | Major version, no tests for state-machine |
| typebox | 1.0.50 -> 1.0.73 | MEDIUM | Patch version, tested via type.spec.ts |
| @types/node | 22.x -> 25.x | MEDIUM | Major version, but limited Node API usage |
| turbo | 2.3.0 -> 2.7.2 | LOW | Build tool, minor version |
| @biomejs/biome | 2.3.8 -> 2.3.11 | LOW | Patch version |
| tsup | 8.3.0 -> 8.5.1 | LOW | Minor version |
| vitest | 4.0.8 -> 4.0.16 | LOW | Patch version |

---

## Rollback Strategy

If issues are found:

### Quick Rollback
```bash
git revert a3552f7
pnpm install
```

### Partial Rollback (specific package)
Edit `packages/tools/package.json`:
```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "immer": "^10.2.0"  // Revert if state-machine breaks
  }
}
```

### Immer-Specific Fix
If immer 11.x causes issues with class instances:
```typescript
import { setUseStrictShallowCopy } from 'immer';
setUseStrictShallowCopy('class_only');
```

---

## Execution Checklist

- [ ] Phase 1.1: Clean installation
- [ ] Phase 1.2: Type checking passes
- [ ] Phase 1.3: Linting passes
- [ ] Phase 1.4: Build succeeds
- [ ] Phase 1.5: Existing tests pass
- [ ] Phase 2.2: Manual state machine tests pass
- [ ] Phase 3.2: Custom date type works
- [ ] Phase 5.1: All exports verified
- [ ] Phase 5.2: ESM/CJS dual build works

---

## Recommendations

1. **Add State Machine Tests** - The state-machine module has zero test coverage and is the highest risk area due to immer dependency.

2. **Add Integration Tests** - Create tests that verify the full export surface works correctly.

3. **Consider CI Pipeline** - Add automated testing for package upgrades via dependabot or renovate.

---

## Sources

- [Immer v11.0.0 Release Notes](https://github.com/immerjs/immer/releases/tag/v11.0.0)
- [TypeBox 1.0.0 Migration Guide](https://github.com/sinclairzx81/typebox/blob/main/changelog/1.0.0-migration.md)
- [Node.js v25 Release Notes](https://nodejs.org/en/blog/release/v25.0.0)

---

### Critical Files for Implementation

- `/Users/anotonio.silva/antonio/belt/packages/tools/src/state-machine/slice.ts` - Slice reducers using immer `produce()`, critical for state mutations
